title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating a top-ranked player on the international stage.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open, marking a notable victory in his career.

title: BAI Updates Selection Criteria for Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships
indian_angle: This update provides crucial details for young Indian badminton players aspiring to represent the country internationally.
summary: The Badminton Association of India released a further addendum to the selection criteria for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026, outlining revised guidelines for junior players.

title: BAI Issues Advisory Against Unauthorized Badminton Leagues
indian_angle: The BAI's advisory protects Indian players and the integrity of domestic badminton by warning against unapproved events.
summary: BAI issued an advisory cautioning players, coaches, and organizers against participating in or promoting unauthorized badminton leagues, tournaments, and championships to maintain regulatory standards.

title: PV Sindhu's Early Steps to Stardom Traced in Macau
indian_angle: This feature highlights the early career journey of India's Olympic medalist PV Sindhu, offering insights into her formative years.
summary: An article delves into the early career of Indian badminton star PV Sindhu, detailing her initial steps towards stardom in Macau, often dubbed the 'gambling capital' of the world.

title: Indian Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games; Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The article covers the Indian badminton squad's preparations for the prestigious Asian Games, including key player returns.
summary: The Indian Thomas Cup squad has reassembled to prepare for the Asian Games, with the notable return of women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand to the team.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament Announced for Chennai
indian_angle: This circular announces a significant upcoming domestic ranking tournament for senior Indian badminton players.
summary: BAI has released a circular announcing the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, scheduled to take place in Chennai from July 16th to 23rd.
